messages.author: Art-X
messages.text: I was thinking on how to limit the load on the repeaters. Isn't a simple solution to keep a 'hidden' header to the message with a stacked source region and destination? : suppose we send a message to route from london (UK) to amsterdam (NL) New functionality: Select own Region from a list with all Regions, Flood Ping: repeaters scan direct neighbours and add their main Region their Enable list, Rules: repeater always repeat only in their own enabled regions, ^ means NOT Route example London, Norwich, Harwich, The Hague(NL), Amsterdam HOPS: 0 ^LON:AMS => Still in London, LON will repeat since it's in the enabled region 1 ^LON^NOR:AMS => Border repeater that sees two Regions, only NOR will be repeated, since LON is already in the route list 2 ^LON^NOR:AMS => Repeater with only NOR Repeats NOR, but maintains the route 3 ^LON^NOR^HAR:AMS => Repeater that sees NOR and HAR, will repeat HAR, since NOR is already in the route list 4 ^LON^NOR^HAR^HAG => Repeater that sees HAR and HAG, will repeat HAG, since HAR is already in the route list 5 ^LON^NOR^HAR^HAG^AMS:AMS => Repeater that sees HAG and AMS, since the destination AMS is in the routing it will only repeat AMS (edited)
messages.content_html: I was thinking on how to limit the load on the repeaters. Isn't a simple solution to keep a 'hidden' header to the message with a stacked source region and destination? : suppose we send a message to route from london (UK) to amsterdam (NL) New functionality: Select own Region from a list with all Regions, Flood Ping: repeaters scan direct neighbours and add their main Region their Enable list, Rules: repeater always repeat only in their own enabled regions, ^ means NOT Route example London, Norwich, Harwich, The Hague(NL), Amsterdam HOPS: 0 ^LON:AMS => Still in London, LON will repeat since it's in the enabled region 1 ^LON^NOR:AMS => Border repeater that sees two Regions, only NOR will be repeated, since LON is already in the route list 2 ^LON^NOR:AMS => Repeater with only NOR Repeats NOR, but maintains the route 3 ^LON^NOR^HAR:AMS => Repeater that sees NOR and HAR, will repeat HAR, since NOR is already in the route list 4 ^LON^NOR^HAR^HAG => Repeater that sees HAR and HAG, will repeat HAG, since HAR is already in the route list 5 ^LON^NOR^HAR^HAG^AMS:AMS => Repeater that sees HAG and AMS, since the destination AMS is in the routing it will only repeat AMS (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 1
